title: SSE buffer in stream_sse_response grows unbounded with no newline
description:
## Severity
Critical
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/pipeline.rs:516-543
## Violation
The SSE parsing loop accumulates upstream bytes into an unbounded String until a newline is found. A hostile or broken upstream that streams payloads without newline (or a single very long SSE event) will inflate the buffer until the process OOMs. The inactivity timeout is reset by every received byte, so a steady newline-free stream is welcomed. Also forces a full UTF-8 validation+copy per chunk via String::from_utf8_lossy.
## Mandated refactor
Introduce MAX_SSE_LINE_BYTES and MAX_SSE_BUFFER_BYTES constants; abort and emit AppEvent::ApiError when exceeded. Switch to BytesMut + memchr to avoid per-chunk UTF-8 round-trip. Track total bytes against an absolute ceiling separate from the inactivity timeout.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
